title: Fix missing mutex on createChannel/deleteChannel/createDecision
description:
**Found during QA deep audit of B8/B16 fixes.**

`createChannel()` (line ~665), `deleteChannel()` (line ~886), and `createDecision()` (line ~1026) perform read-check-write sequences without holding `this.mutex`.

**Race scenario for createChannel:**
1. Two concurrent calls with same channel name
2. Both pass the duplicate name check at line ~681
3. Both write the channel — duplicate created

**Fix:** Wrap these methods in `this.mutex.runExclusive()` like `pinMessage`/`unpinMessage` already are.

Also document the lock ordering constraint: `this.mutex` → `channelMutex` (never reverse). `claimNextTask` holds `this.mutex` and calls `sendMessage` which acquires `channelMutex` — this ordering must be preserved.
definitionOfDone: createChannel, deleteChannel, createDecision wrapped in mutex.runExclusive
Lock ordering documented in code comment
No concurrent createChannel can create duplicates
